remember: Students will be able to recall and identify the general properties of alcohols, phenols, and ethers.
Students will be able to list the physical and chemical properties of different types of alcohols, phenols, and ethers.
Students will be able to recall the general methods of preparation of alcohols, phenols, and ethers.
understand: Students will be able to understand the structural features of alcohols, phenols, and ethers that influence their properties and reactivity.
Students will be able to explain the differences in the physical and chemical properties of alcohols, phenols, and ethers due to their structures.
Students will be able to understand the reaction mechanisms of various reactions involving alcohols, phenols, and ethers.
apply: Students will be able to apply their knowledge of alcohols, phenols, and ethers to predict their reactivity in various reactions.
Students will be able to use their understanding of the structural features of alcohols, phenols, and ethers to design syntheses and predict the outcome of reactions.
Students will be able to apply their knowledge to identify and separate mixtures of alcohols, phenols, and ethers using various techniques.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the data from experiments involving alcohols, phenols, and ethers to draw conclusions about their properties and reactivity.
Students will be able to analyze the structural features of alcohols, phenols, and ethers to predict their reactivity and design syntheses.
Students will be able to analyze the results of reactions involving alcohols, phenols, and ethers to identify patterns and trends.

ncert_objectives: To study the general properties and methods of preparation of alcohols, phenols, and ethers.
To understand the structural features and reactivity of alcohols, phenols, and ethers.
To study the reactions involving alcohols, phenols, and ethers and their applications.
board_exam_objectives: To identify and classify different types of alcohols, phenols, and ethers.
To describe the physical and chemical properties of alcohols, phenols, and ethers.
To explain the methods of preparation of alcohols, phenols, and ethers.
To predict the reactivity of alcohols, phenols, and ethers in various reactions.
To describe the applications of alcohols, phenols, and ethers in various industries.
